learning_outcomes.description: To understand and apply various software programs for analysis of biomedical literature and data
To create a scientific question and design experiments for testing a hypothesis
To critically evaluate scientific data and previously published work
To communicate research findings through written, oral, poster formats to diverse audiences
To work effectively in a research team, identify the characteristics of effective teamwork and critically evaluate personal and peer contributions to team processes
To evaluate ethical issues in biomedical research and apply culturally inclusive, responsible, and ethical conduct in their research practice

assessments.due_date:
Week 1 diary entry 31/07/2026 3:00 pm

Week 2 diary entry 7/08/2026 3:00 pm

Week 3 diary entry 14/08/2026 3:00 pm

Week 4 diary entry 21/08/2026 3:00 pm

Week 5 diary entry 28/08/2026 3:00 pm

Workshop diary entries are to be submitted at the end of each week (Friday at 3 pm). The final collated diary for weeks 1-5 must be submitted in week 5.
assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Participation/ Student contribution, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, you are required to complete a workshop diary entry at the end of each workshop throughout the first five weeks of the course. The diary is designed to encourage reflection on your learning, consolidate key concepts, and identify areas for further development.

Each week, you will respond to a series of questions related to the workshop topics and learning activities.

The workshop diary contributes 25% of your final grade. Your diary will be reviewed weekly by your instructors, with 5% allocated to each week's entry. Assessment will consider the quality of your reflections, your engagement with the workshop activities, and your participation and contribution throughout the course.

Your completed workshop diary (diary entries from weeks 1-5 collated) must be submitted via Blackboard at the end of Week 5 (in the Week 5 submission point).

More details about this assessment will be provided during the semester via classes and Blackboard.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must submit your Assessment task, with the electronic coversheet available on the School's website (under Undergraduate - Assessment), to Turnitin by the submission deadline. You may submit drafts to Turnitin up to the due date, but you will only be able to submit once on or after the due date. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Experimental design and research practices test
assessments.task_description:
For this in-class written test, you will be assessed on experimental design concepts and research practices covered in the first 3 weeks of the course.

Have your UQ student ID card available for all in-person assessments and exams.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This 60-minute in-class written test will assess your understanding of the experimental design concepts and research practices covered during the first three weeks of the course. Specifically, the test will cover; (1) Experimental design, (2) Ethics in biomed research, and (3) Data presentation and describing figures. The test will be completed in class and will consist of a combination of multiple-choice and short-answer questions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
For the assessment, you will prepare and present a scientific poster that critically evaluates a recent high-impact biomedical research publication. You will identify a recent publication relevant to your field (not authored by your host lab team), critically evaluate the study's experimental design, data and analyses, prepare a scientific poster summarising the key findings and their significance, and present your poster to a panel of UQ biomedical science academics.

The assessment comprises two equally weighted components: Poster PDF (25%), Poster Oral presentation (25%)

Submissions of the Poster PDF must occur prior to oral assessment. Students who do not submit their Poster PDFs prior to the deadline are not eligible to take part in the oral assessment and must apply for an extension/deferred assessment. No changes to the Poster PDF are permitted after the oral assessment.

More details about this assessment will be provided during the semester via classes and Blackboard.

Have your UQ student ID card available for all in-person assessments and exams.

As this assessment is marked independently by two or more examiners and undergoes a post-marking moderation process, re-marks are not permitted.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.

assessments.task_description:
For this assessment, you are required to write a brief seminar reflection report on an approved 1-hour biomedical science seminar you have attended during the semester. Your report should summarise the seminar's key points and learnings, critical evaluation of the research presented including techniques and innovation, communication and presentation style, as well as relevance to your research area of interest. The report will assess your written communication skills.

The report length is maximum of 800 words. Further assessment details will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.

learning_activities.topic:
Getting to know you and resources for biomedical researchers

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:

  1. To promote networking and collaboration amongst student cohort
  2. To train students to work effectively in teams
  3. To be able to conduct scientific research in an ethical, integral, equitable and inclusive manner
  4. To understand the opportunities and responsibilities of using AI


LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

SESSION 1A: Course overview; Honours goal setting and myth busters (with panel of recent honours graduates)

SESSION 1B: Introduction to research ethics and ethical use of AI; Cultural Considerations in Research; Generating Graphical Abstracts


Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Getting to know you and resources for biomedical researchers
learning_activities.topic_description: Getting to know you and resources for biomedical researchers LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To promote networking and collaboration amongst student cohort To train students to work effectively in teams To be able to conduct scientific research in an ethical, integral, equitable and inclusive manner To understand the opportunities and responsibilities of using AI LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 1A: Course overview; Honours goal setting and myth busters (with panel of recent honours graduates) SESSION 1B: Introduction to research ethics and ethical use of AI; Cultural Considerations in Research; Generating Graphical Abstracts

learning_activities.topic:
Experimental Design and Research Practices

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:

  1. To design a scientific question, research hypothesis, experimental aims, and craft an informative abstract
  2. To familiarise students with relevant software programs to support biomedical research 
  3. To familiarise students with scientific communication through poster and oral formats


LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

SESSION 2B: Introduction to experimental design; Brief introduction/exercise relating to GraphPad Prism, Endnote, and Pubmed; Team activity exploring other useful resources for biomedical researchers 

SESSION 2B: Art of oral presentations and ECR Showcase. Poster Gallery Walk and Power Hour


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Experimental Design and Research Practices LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To design a scientific question, research hypothesis, experimental aims, and craft an informative abstract To familiarise students with relevant software programs to support biomedical research To familiarise students with scientific communication through poster and oral formats LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 2B: Introduction to experimental design; Brief introduction/exercise relating to GraphPad Prism, Endnote, and Pubmed; Team activity exploring other useful resources for biomedical researchers SESSION 2B: Art of oral presentations and ECR Showcase. Poster Gallery Walk and Power Hour

learning_activities.topic:
Critical analysis for biomedical scientists

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:

  1. To critically evaluate scientific data and literature, assessing credibility and reliability
  2. To develop skills in formulating and asking questions 


LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

SESSION 3A: Editorial review of selected scientific papers in teams

 SESSION 3B: EKKA Show Day (no classes)


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Critical analysis for biomedical scientists LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To critically evaluate scientific data and literature, assessing credibility and reliability To develop skills in formulating and asking questions LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 3A: Editorial review of selected scientific papers in teams SESSION 3B: EKKA Show Day (no classes)

learning_activities.topic:
Scientific communication and audience targeting

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:

  1. To develop scientific communication skills using various formats, written, oral, poster
  2. To hone skills in presenting to various audiences, including lay  
  3. To familiarise students in the art of crafting research proposals


LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

SESSION 4A: Introduction to writing research proposals; Mock assessment of exemplar theses in teams and class discussions 

SESSION 4B: Tailoring your message to match your audience – group communication task tailoring key messages to diverse audiences (government, community, science peers); Mock Poster presentations and feedback


Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Scientific communication and audience targeting LEARNING OBJECTIVES: To develop scientific communication skills using various formats, written, oral, poster To hone skills in presenting to various audiences, including lay To familiarise students in the art of crafting research proposals LEARNING ACTIVITIES: SESSION 4A: Introduction to writing research proposals; Mock assessment of exemplar theses in teams and class discussions SESSION 4B: Tailoring your message to match your audience – group communication task tailoring key messages to diverse audiences (government, community, science peers); Mock Poster presentations and feedback

learning_activities.topic:
Connecting and communicating as biomedical scientists 

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:

  1. To develop SMART skills for research project management
  2. To establish an engaging online presence
  3. To identify and get the most out of career mentors


LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

SESSION 5A: Smart skills and soft skills for biomedical researchers; Referee activity; Setting up an engaging LinkedIn or BlueSky account

SESSION 5B: Final Poster Presentation Showcase


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
